subject: This is different.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
The real work is about to start.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7accd/2f5ea88d/7adb416f/2cbda622/32238304/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-6_freeze-1&refcode=ema_2018-12-6_freeze-1&utm_campaign=freeze-1&utm_medium=ema") Friend, Next month, the next phase of our critical work is going to start. The upcoming legislative session in Washington is going to be different. We showed up strong in the election, and now our state legislature is inarguably more progressive. I'm proud of Washington voters, and I am excited about what we can accomplish. I already know what's on our plate -- and I know we'll make some real headway:
* Next week we will roll out a comprehensive climate and clean energy agenda putting Washington at the vanguard of the nation in combating climate change.
* We are going to implement the best paid family and medical leave in the country in 2019 -- meaning that by 2020, employees will be able to take up to 12 weeks to care for their family and loved ones or to recover from an illness.
* We will also continue to transform our behavioral health system to provide more personalized, effective care so our citizens can live their fullest lives.

...And so much more. But before the start of the session, we'll enter a mandatory fundraising freeze. **There's never been a more important time to show that we are united -- and that we have the strength to pull all of this off.** Starting midnight on December 14th, we will no longer be able to accept donations, so the time to pull out all the stops and prove that we are a force to be reckoned with starts now. [**Before our freeze starts at midnight on the 14th, we're setting a goal of 2,000 donations. Will you be one of those donations right now and prove we're going to succeed this session?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7accd/2f5ea88d/7adb416f/2cbda622/32238304/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-6_freeze-1&refcode=ema_2018-12-6_freeze-1&utm_campaign=freeze-1&utm_medium=ema") I know we can do it -- but only with your help. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7accd/2f5ea88d/7adb416f/2cbda622/32238304/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-6_freeze-1&refcode=ema_2018-12-6_freeze-1&utm_campaign=freeze-1&utm_medium=ema")

subject: Hanukkah at the White House
body:
This time of year marks a cherished Jewish tradition as families come together for eight nights to participate in the lighting of the menorah. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3bff6776de&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • December 6, 2018
---
| | |
# **Hanukkah at the White House**

**This time of year** marks a cherished Jewish tradition as families come together for eight nights to light the menorah. The tradition began more than 2,000 years ago during the rededication of the Holy Temple in Jerusalem, which followed a trying period when Jews were persecuted for practicing their faith.

**Today,** the White House is hosting a pair of Hanukkah receptions in celebration of this special time of year. “Melania and I send our warmest greetings to our Jewish brothers and sisters in the United States, in Israel, and around the world celebrating Hanukkah,” President Donald J. Trump said earlier this week.

**In his Hanukkah message,** President Trump called on Americans to remember the victims at the Tree of Life Synagogue in Pittsburgh whose lives were cut short this past October. “As one Nation, we pledge our continued love and support for the victims, their families, and the community, and we pray that the victims’ families find some measure of peace and comfort during this holiday season,” he said.

**“Over the coming days,** may the warming glow of each candle on the menorah help fill homes and hearts with love and happiness.”

[Watch President Trump deliver remarks at a White House Hanukkah reception.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5ae92857f6&e=3ee4985eea)

**Read:** [The President’s 2018 Message for Hanukkah](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=41f36e296d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Video of the day: A historic anniversary**

**One year ago today,** President Trump announced to the world that the United States would at last move its embassy in Israel to the country’s rightful capital of Jerusalem.

**“While previous presidents** have made this a major campaign promise, they failed to deliver. Today, I am delivering,” President Trump said.

[Watch President Trump make the historic announcement.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=034192d2dd&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Looking back: First Lady Melania Trump at the G20 Summit**

**Last weekend,** First Lady Melania Trump accompanied the President to the 2018 G20 Summit in Buenos Aires, Argentina. With a number of world leaders assembled, the First Lady participated in a series of events hosted by Juliana Awada, the First Lady of Argentina.

**On Saturday,** Mrs. Trump attended the official spousal program at the Buenos Aires Museum of Latin American Art, which houses a collection of 400 pieces. The First Lady joined the other partners for a presentation by the First Lady of Argentina, who spoke on the importance of Argentina’s National Plan on Early Childhood.

**Mrs. Trump signed a chair** designed by a local artist at the end of the presentation, showing support for Argentina’s early childhood programs—an area of focus similar to her own [Be Best initiative](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3da7b51014&e=3ee4985eea). “Thank you to both President Macri and Ms. Awada for the wonderful hospitality,” the First Lady said.

[Watch First Lady Melania Trump at the G20 Summit in Argentina](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=614bf776e2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|

I am so grateful for your faith in me, and I hope you will consider continuing to support our cause as we enter the next election cycle. body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/3558835/25822386/368741054?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NmQ3MzNmMC00MWZhLWU4MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4Nzg0ZjliNjUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bI5X1NLzNhwaikPfEaT79KLX05iLcLkxAN7caG7R8yY=)
Peter,

As the most senior member from Illinois on the House Transportation and Infrastructure Committee, I have brought over $375 million in federal funding to Illinois’ roads, bridges, and transportation projects. This is an issue I care deeply about, and one that is highly important to Illinois residents.

**Today I’m rolling up my sleeves to do even more to fix our nation’s infrastructure._[If you’re with us, please click here to help us launch our Infrastructure Campaign.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/3558837/25822388/74905028?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NmQ3MzNmMC00MWZhLWU4MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4Nzg0ZjliNjUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bI5X1NLzNhwaikPfEaT79KLX05iLcLkxAN7caG7R8yY=&refcode=DL_EM_FR_90DO_181207_1_1&amount=10&recurring=auto)_**

I was recently named the co-chair of Governor-Elect J.B. Pritzker’s Restoring Illinois’ Infrastructure Committee, and I am proud to be able to work with a dedicated team to find commonsense solutions to repair our crumbling infrastructure.

This should be top priority. A recent report found that over a third of Illinois’ roads are in poor or mediocre condition. Thanks to headache-inducing congestion and our deteriorating roads and bridges, drivers in Illinois alone lose an estimated $16.4 billion annually. It’s time to tackle this problem head-on.

**_[That’s why my team is launching our Infrastructure Campaign, but we need 100 grassroots donors to step up and help out. Can you chip in $10 now to start us off?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/3558838/25822390/74905028?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NmQ3MzNmMC00MWZhLWU4MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4Nzg0ZjliNjUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bI5X1NLzNhwaikPfEaT79KLX05iLcLkxAN7caG7R8yY=&refcode=DL_EM_FR_90DO_181207_1_1&amount=10&recurring=auto)_**

We need to move ahead on this at both the federal and state levels to fix our crumbling infrastructure, improve our quality of life and put people to work.

I'm pleased to have a seat at the table for both efforts, and I’m asking you to help us lay out the groundwork for huge infrastructure improvements starting right now.

**_[If you’re ready for better roads and bridges, click here to help us launch our Infrastructure Campaign.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/3558839/25822392/74905028?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NmQ3MzNmMC00MWZhLWU4MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4Nzg0ZjliNjUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bI5X1NLzNhwaikPfEaT79KLX05iLcLkxAN7caG7R8yY=&refcode=DL_EM_FR_90DO_181207_1_1&amount=10&recurring=auto)_**

Thanks,

Dan

name: Jason Yates
subject: SCOTUS Watch #8
body:
| | Critical Updates from the current SCOTUS session
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ie-EuEwprJECnklQBd9pnr135GwU4Ftlb5QHSyBEa-1meL68NXf6X7rAXyASfKiWlA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181207com01&n=1)
| The Supreme Court began hearing cases for the 2018/2019 term on October 1, 2018 and concluded hearing cases for the year on December 6, 2018. The Supreme Court heard a total of 33 cases from October – December 2018. **Supreme Court Rejects Appeal to Stop Trump’s Border Wall** On Monday the Supreme Court rejected environmental groups appeals to prohibit President Trump from building a border wall. The environmentalists claimed the Trump administration improperly waived laws requiring environmentalists to review the project before beginning construction. But a San Diego federal judge rejected this claimed and the Supreme Court upheld his ruling. **Supreme Court Decisions:** The Supreme Court issued opinions on two cases so far this term: **1\. Mount Lemmon Fire District v. Guido on November 6**
* “The court ruled that the Age Discrimination in Employment Act (ADEA) applies to all public employers, including those with fewer than 20 employees.” _[read more](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=RDRHk6ni7OvEv8Bu9zj6hMZuPHpu2jkJY5OXY9l0fuD_g9JIFnhZPlIRL2GU_6lOTXJjygcOclNodLlb6eKDxQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181207com01&n=2)_

**2\. Weyerhaeuser Company v. U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service on November 27**
* “The United States Supreme Court held 8-0 to vacate the decision from the [Fifth Circuit](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=RDRHk6ni7OvEv8Bu9zj6hKGaJATmaYgTaOxS-oNKk-AJOMKdF9qYMFPax8kNn50p3w1YKZxGdL92lTsfFKuKcJIz1saP834ae2q3vBkVWcg&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181207com01&n=3) and remand the case. The Supreme Court instructed the Fifth Circuit to consider in the first instance whether the FWS' critical habitat designation was arbitrary, capricious, or an abuse of discretion.” _[read more](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=RDRHk6ni7OvEv8Bu9zj6hKk7n_wXeztFWMg53iB8FLkh0fa-HnCS0okvaMI9XdURlYCNqS1S3SdyYyr0hfRAJUAUiYRol5aP-k50X_Qe6M3dVuB7rUuotkQiHwfBxH5y&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181207com01&n=4)_

**Supreme Court Case Update:** Below are three interesting cases the Supreme Court recently heard oral arguments for that are worth highlighting. **Case 1: Frank v. Gaos** Google was sued by a group of people in 2013 for providing users’ internet search terms to third-party groups. This is a violation of privacy rights under the U.S. Stored Communications Act. Google agreed to pay $8.5 million to settle the dispute. $3.2 million paid lawyer fees for the three named plaintiffs and the remaining $5.3 million was paid as a cy pres payment to six organizations. A cy pres recipient is a class action judgment or settlement where the funds are given to nonprofit organizations that indirectly help the class members harmed, which means the 129 million Google users whose privacy was breached received nothing in this settlement. In this case, Google paid the money to universities and organizations providing education on internet privacy. The court preliminarily approved this agreement but individuals in 2014, including Thomas Frank, challenged the plan. The court moved forward anyway with the settlement in 2015. The central question in this case: [Whether, or in what circumstances, a cy pres award of class action proceeds that provides no direct relief to class members supports class certification and comports with the requirement that a settlement binding class members must be "fair, reasonable, and adequate."](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=RDRHk6ni7OvEv8Bu9zj6hOa_xUqd96L8P19HQWtp4TLOmIHgCILs0ENAWAqU16jd&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181207com01&n=5) Oral arguments for this case were heard on October 31, 2018. **Case 2: Bucklew v. Precythe** In 1996 Russell Bucklew was convicted of kidnapping, murder, and rape. A jury sentenced him to receive the death penalty for his crimes. In 2014 Bucklew filed an action claiming that execution by lethal injection would be a cruel punishment because he claimed to have a rare medical condition, cavernous hemangioma. He requested to be executed by nitrogen hypoxia. Additionally, he questioned if Missouri’s lethal injection team were qualified to conduct his execution. The lower court and the Eighth Circuit District Court all ruled against his claims yet the case caused many questions to arise. The Supreme Court voted 5 to 4 on his case back in March to delay his execution and to hear the challenges of Bucklew’s medical condition as it relates to lethal injection. Judge Brett Kavanaugh is assumed to have the deciding vote on this case. The central questions in this case: [Whether the Eighth Amendment requires an inmate to prove an adequate alternative method of execution when raising an as-applied challenge to the state’s proposed method of execution based on his rare and severe medical condition. ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=RDRHk6ni7OvEv8Bu9zj6hCsCI1ZsLAECmdCxLLnaIJezsKdxiuidGe2nDfwrp50n&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181207com01&n=6)You can read the other three questions central to the case [here](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=RDRHk6ni7OvEv8Bu9zj6hCsCI1ZsLAECmdCxLLnaIJezsKdxiuidGe2nDfwrp50n&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181207com01&n=7). Oral arguments for this case were heard on November 6, 2018. **Case 3: Gamble v. United States** In 2008 Terance Martez Gamble was found guilty of second-degree robbery and convicted in Alabama state court of a felony. In 2015 Gamble was pulled over by an Alabama police officer that found marijuana and a 9-millimeter pistol in his car. This is a clear violation of the law under both Alabama and federal law for a felon to possess a firearm. The Alabama court sentenced him to one year in prison and then a federal district court sentenced Gamble to 46 months in prison. Gamble appealed the ruling arguing that since Alabama had already prosecuted him for the crime, it would be against the double jeopardy clause of the Fifth Amendment for the federal government to prosecute him for the same crime. The Fifth Amendment states no “person [shall] be subject for the same offense to be twice put in jeopardy of life or limb.” However, there has long been an understanding of the rule “separate sovereigns” for both the federal government and state government. A ruling on this case will set an important precedent moving forward. The central question in this case: [Whether the Court should overrule the "separate sovereigns" exception to the Double Jeopardy Clause](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=RDRHk6ni7OvEv8Bu9zj6hC8XlUM-1Bivti-4r8nnGYNv-XsgZ3W81n8gWyK5DfK3&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181207com01&n=8). Oral arguments for this case were heard on December 6, 2018.
* * *
[Support My Faith Votes with your tax deductible donation.](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66idsHszZ_cNjdrkrk6Ct22R5LqpO-pYuvVIOOj4RsxM_Q&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181207com01&n=9) _My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement focused on motivating Christians in America to participate in local and national elections and be active members of society. By partnering with local churches, pastors, and national faith leaders, My Faith Votes mobilizes and equips Christians to lead the conversation on the place of faith in culture and politics._

body:

| | Bankrupt Sears wants court approval to pay executives $25 million in bonuses. This is happening at the same time they're closing hundreds of stores and laying off thousands of dedicated hourly employees who worked in them. Many of these hourly workers don't even think they'll be paid severance. **This is a disgraceful, ridiculous example of the culture of corruption and greed that permeates corporate America.** Executives who drive companies into the ground, break them up and/or ship American jobs overseas are handed huge checks while their workers are handed pink slips. Congress needs to do more to protect American workers and their families. I will fight for every worker -- no matter their zip code, economic status, sexual orientation, or the color of their skin. [**If you're with me, let me know by adding your name.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffda5/4462dc26/89b82dd7/201ef568/1619389620/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181207z") Whether you work for an hourly wage at a Sears store or tips in a restaurant, you deserve to be treated with respect. Too many leaders in Washington have forgotten that. **I won't forget.** I'm organizing support around a system that rewards work -- not one that hands failed executives $25 million in bonuses. [**Sign my petition, add your voice to thousands more, and let 's get to work.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffda5/4462dc26/89b82dd7/201ef568/1619389620/VEsE/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181207z") I hope I can continue to count on your support. | | | [I'M WITH YOU!](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffda5/4462dc26/89b82dd7/201ef568/1619389620/VEsF/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181207z")

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump Celebrates Hanukkah at the White House
body:
President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump led two Hanukkah receptions in the White House East Room yesterday... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5d12e64c5f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Celebrates Hanukkah at the White House**

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump led two Hanukkah receptions in the White House East Room yesterday, celebrating the fifth night of the Festival of Lights.

“In his first of two Hanukkah events, Mr. Trump remembered the life of former President George H.W. Bush, calling him a wonderful man. He went on to condemn anti-Semitism, specifically mentioning the mass shooting at the Tree of Life synagogue,” CBS News reports. The receptions came exactly one year after President Trump announced that the U.S. Embassy in Israel would be moved to Jerusalem.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=38e48f7946&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| In _[Bloomberg](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ae4624c843&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Javier Blas reports that “America turned into a net oil exporter last week, breaking almost 75 years of continued dependence on foreign oil” and marking a pivotal moment in President Trump’s promise to become energy independent. “The shift to net exports is the dramatic result of an unprecedented boom in American oil production, with thousands of wells pumping.”
---
|
---
| “An illegal immigrant arrested for allegedly shooting and killing a co-worker in Oregon is discovered to have been deported at least eight times,” Jason Hopkins reports for _[The Daily Signal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=731c9461ab&e=3ee4985eea)_. The suspect is currently being held in the Umatilla County Sheriff’s Office, where he “is being charged with one count of murder, one count of felon in possession of a weapon, and one count of INS detainer. His bail has been set at $5,000,000.”
---
|
---
| “President Donald J. Trump has been the subject of criticism recently because of some confusion about a recent agreement between China and the United States on trade. Although the media will not cut the president a break, as time has worn on, the agreement with China seems likely to be yet another trade victory for President Trump,” Corey Lewandowski writes in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=473f32dd5f&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---
|
---
| In _[The Washington Times](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bb736fc375&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Stephen Dinan writes that yesterday the Senate voted to confirm Kathleen Kraninger as the new director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. “She has promised to stay the course set by Mr. Mulvaney, saying her priorities will be to bring transparency and accountability to the agency, and to protect consumer privacy while launching ‘aggressive’ action against bad actors.”

body:
They are showing spiteful partisan pandering.

Republicans are reversing the will of the people -- putting politics ahead of our democracy. I wish we could chalk this up to a bunch of sore losers, but it's far more insidious. It's anti-democratic. And there's only one reason for the Republicans' actions: Progressives won the governorship and critical ballot measures in their states, fair and square. **We have to tell them this is wrong and show we won't stand for this. Not for a second.** [**It's time to take action, right here and right now. Sign my petition to demand Wisconsin and Michigan Republicans stop subverting the will of the people.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ac10/2f5ea88d/7af47c54/2cbda10f/802808055/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/wi-and-mi-gov-powers?utm_source=ema_2018-12-07_gov-power&source=ema_2018-12-07_gov-power&utm_campaign=gov-power&utm_medium=ema") Again and again, we've seen Republicans far too willing to play politics with our democratic institutions. Throughout the elections in state after state, Republicans did their best to suppress voters, making it difficult for citizens to make their voices heard. Their goal was simple -- to stay in power regardless of the cost. Republicans have been pulling increasingly shady moves, like this, to weaken progressive power. Now that voters have kicked them out of office, they're desperate -- and they're trying their best to disrespect the decisions their constituents made. [**Tell Wisconsin and Michigan Republicans: Our democratic institutions are too important for your political games. And our democracy is more important than your partisan politics. body:
One week ago today, our nation lost a great America hero. President George H.W. Bush was a man who devoted his life to our nation, to his family,... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/honoring-41?e=1acd9bb3d6)

| **_Honoring 41_** One week ago today, our nation lost a true American hero. **President George H.W. Bush** was a man who devoted his life to our nation, to his family, and to making the world a brighter, better place. We will all miss him.

I'll always remember during his speech accepting the Republican nomination for president in 1988, **President Bush** described "a thousand points of light in a broad and peaceful sky." It's an inspiring image -- Americans, groups and organizations everywhere acting as points of light and hope in making the world a better place.

We've got so many points of light right here in Indiana, and that includes all of you. Whether you're a leader of your local Rotary Club, volunteer at your church, or help coach your child's youth sports team, you are a point of light and you are helping live out **President Bush** 's vision.

During Wednesday's funeral, our other **President Bush** , his son, again evoked that same light when describing his father. "To us, his was the brightest of a thousand points of light."

If you haven't had a chance to watch**President George W. Bush's** eulogy to his father, I'd highly encourage you to take a few minutes and [watch it here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3d3977ef98&e=1acd9bb3d6).

**George H.W. Bush** was a distinguished American -- a World War II veteran, a former congressman, an ambassador to the UN and China, a chairman of the RNC, a CIA director, vice president, and of course, President of the United States.

Let's remember **President George H.W. Bush** , and let's never forget to strive to be points of light in our own communities. -Kyle Hupfer
---
|

---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=45fdc5fef7&e=1acd9bb3d6)

| **_Announcing Governor Holcomb's 2019 Next Level Agenda_** We all know that **Governor Eric Holcomb** is dedicated to making Indiana the best state in the nation to live, work and play -- and there's no question that he's taking that objective, well, to the Next Level with his 2019 Next Level Agenda.

You can read the [full agenda here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=547dd43409&e=1acd9bb3d6), which he announced yesterday. Here are some highlights, which follow his five governing pillars:

**1\. Cultivate a Strong & Diverse Economy**
-Pass Indiana's eighth-straight balanced budget (with responsible reserves!)
-Modernize economic development tools to increase competitiveness, economic diversity and flexibility
-Exempt military pensions from state income tax to attract & retain more veterans to the state

**2\. Maintain & Build Indiana's Infrastructure**
-Complete I-69 three years early (by 2024)
-Expand access to high-speed, affordable broadband to unserved communities

**3\. Support Education & Workforce Development**
-Give schools flexibility to develop partnerships to expose students to more opportunities
-Expand Workforce Ready Grants & Employer Training Grants that are making Next Level Jobs a big success

**4\. Promote Public Health & Attack the Drug Epidemic**
-Adopt school safety recommendations, including more mental health tools and physical safety enhancements
-Continue to make improvements at the Department of Child Services
-Provide new tools to lower the infant mortality rate and help those recovering from the drug epidemic

**5\. Deliver Great Government Service**
-Modernize state government technology
-Streamline state boards and commissions to make it easier for Hoosiers to interact with their government

| **_Now Hiring: Intern for the Indiana GOP!_** Looking for an internship? Know a college student who loves politics?

The Indiana Republican Party is currently [accepting resumes](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ee4e48c7c5&e=1acd9bb3d6) for students interested in an internship during the Spring or Summer 2019 semesters! With paid opportunities available, we’re looking for college students interested in gaining professional experience or getting their start in politics.

Visit [Indiana.gop/internships](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=796b61e0a5&e=1acd9bb3d6) to apply for internships in our political, finance and communications departments, and feel free to reach out to Derek Hugo, Indiana GOP internship coordinator, at [dhugo@indiana.gop](mailto:dhugo@indiana.gop) with any questions.
